input.prompt:
I have a new C# MCP server project. I need to implement a tool class that
wraps a REST API using HttpClient. The tools should follow MCP SDK conventions
with proper attributes for LLM discovery. Show me the tool class, Program.cs
with stdio transport, and explain how DI works for MCP tools.

data.content:
I have a new C# MCP server project. I need to implement a tool class that
wraps a REST API using HttpClient. The tools should follow MCP SDK conventions
with proper attributes for LLM discovery. Show me the tool class, Program.cs
with stdio transport, and explain how DI works for MCP tools.

input.initialPrompt:
I have a new C# MCP server project. I need to implement a tool class that
wraps a REST API using HttpClient. The tools should follow MCP SDK conventions
with proper attributes for LLM discovery. Show me the tool class, Program.cs
with stdio transport, and explain how DI works for MCP tools.

arguments.command: mkdir -p /tmp/sv-0d20b5cce94842e2bee27296c7f38b3c/McpApiServer && cd /tmp/sv-0d20b5cce94842e2bee27296c7f38b3c/McpApiServer && dotnet new console -n McpApiServer --output . --force 2>&1 | tail -3

data.reasoningText:
The user wants me to create a C# MCP (Model Context Protocol) server project with:
1. A tool class wrapping a REST API using HttpClient
2. Program.cs with stdio transport
3. Explanation of how DI works for MCP tools

Let me create a practical example. I'll set up the project structure and explain the patterns.

toolResult.sessionLog:
diff --git a/tmp/sv-0d20b5cce94842e2bee27296c7f38b3c/McpApiServer/WeatherTools.cs b/tmp/sv-0d20b5cce94842e2bee27296c7f38b3c/McpApiServer/WeatherTools.cs
create file mode 100644
index 0000000..0000000
--- a/dev/null
+++ b/tmp/sv-0d20b5cce94842e2bee27296c7f38b3c/McpApiServer/WeatherTools.cs
@@ -1,0 +1,35 @@
+using System.ComponentModel;
+using ModelContextProtocol.Server;
+
+namespace McpApiServer;
+
+/// <summary>
+/// MCP tool class. The SDK discovers [McpServerToolType] classes at startup
+/// and registers every [McpServerTool] method as a callable tool.
+///
+/// DI integration:  Tool methods can accept DI-registered services as
+/// parameters alongside the MCP-provided ones.  The SDK resolves them
+/// from the host's IServiceProvider per-invocation.  Parameters decorated
+/// with [Description] are exposed to the LLM; other parameters (like
+/// WeatherApiClient below) are filled via DI and hidden from the schema.
+/// </summary>
+[McpServerToolType]
+public static class WeatherTools
+{
+    [McpServerTool, Description("Get current weather for a location.")]
+    public static async Task<string> GetCurrentWeather(
+        [Description("Latitude of the location")] double latitude,
+        [Description("Longitude of the location")] double longitude,
+        WeatherApiClient weatherClient,
+        CancellationToken ct)
+    {
+        var weather = await weatherClient.GetCurrentWeatherAsync(latitude, longitude, ct);
+
+        if (weather?.Current is null)
+            return "Unable to retrieve weather data.";
+
+        return $"Temperature: {weather.Current.Temperature}°C, "
+             + $"Wind speed: {weather.Current.WindSpeed} km/h";
+    }
+}
+

index 0000000..0000000
--- a/dev/null
+++ b/tmp/sv-0d20b5cce94842e2bee27296c7f38b3c/McpApiServer/WeatherTools.cs
@@ -1,0 +1,35 @@
+using System.ComponentModel;
+using ModelContextProtocol.Server;
+
+namespace McpApiServer;
+
+/// <summary>
+/// MCP tool class. The SDK discovers [McpServerToolType] classes at startup
+/// and registers every [McpServerTool] method as a callable tool.
+///
+/// DI integration:  Tool methods can accept DI-registered services as
+/// parameters alongside the MCP-provided ones.  The SDK resolves them
+/// from the host's IServiceProvider per-invocation.  Parameters decorated
+/// with [Description] are exposed to the LLM; other parameters (like
+/// WeatherApiClient below) are filled via DI and hidden from the schema.
+/// </summary>
+[McpServerToolType]
+public static class WeatherTools
+{
+    [McpServerTool, Description("Get current weather for a location.")]
+    public static async Task<string> GetCurrentWeather(
+        [Description("Latitude of the location")] double latitude,
+        [Description("Longitude of the location")] double longitude,
+        WeatherApiClient weatherClient,
+        CancellationToken ct)
+    {
+        var weather = await weatherClient.GetCurrentWeatherAsync(latitude, longitude, ct);
+
+        if (weather?.Current is null)
+            return "Unable to retrieve weather data.";
+
+        return $"Temperature: {weather.Current.Temperature}°C, "
+             + $"Wind speed: {weather.Current.WindSpeed} km/h";
+    }
+}
+

data.content:
Build succeeds. Here's the full project:

---

### Program.cs — stdio transport + DI wiring

```csharp
var builder = Host.CreateApplicationBuilder(args);

builder.Services
    .AddHttpClient<WeatherApiClient>(client =>
    {
        client.BaseAddress = new Uri("https://api.open-meteo.com/");
    });

builder.Services
    .AddMcpServer()
    .WithStdioServerTransport()      // reads JSON-RPC from stdin/stdout
    .WithToolsFromAssembly();         // discovers [McpServerToolType] classes

await builder.Build().RunAsync();
```

### WeatherTools.cs — the tool class

```csharp
[McpServerToolType]
public static class WeatherTools
{
    [McpServerTool, Description("Get current weather for a location.")]
    public static async Task<string> GetCurrentWeather(
        [Description("Latitude of the location")]  double latitude,
        [Description("Longitude of the location")] double longitude,
        WeatherApiClient weatherClient,   // ← injected via DI, hidden from LLM
        CancellationToken ct)             // ← injected by runtime
    { ... }
}
```

### WeatherApiClient.cs — typed HttpClient

Registered with `AddHttpClient<WeatherApiClient>(...)` so the DI container manages `HttpClient` lifetime (pooling, DNS rotation, etc.).

---

### How DI works for MCP tools

The SDK splits tool method parameters into two categories:

| Kind | How the SDK identifies it | Visible to LLM? |
|---|---|---|
| **Tool parameters** | Has `[Description]` attribute | ✅ Yes — appears in the JSON schema sent to the LLM |
| **DI services** | Any parameter type registered in the `IServiceProvider` (e.g. `WeatherApiClient`) | ❌ No — resolved from the container per-invocation |
| **Special types** | `CancellationToken`, `IMcpServer` | ❌ No — injected by the MCP runtime |

So when the LLM calls `GetCurrentWeather`, it only provides `latitude` and `longitude`. The SDK resolves `WeatherApiClient` from DI and passes the request's `CancellationToken` automatically. No constructor injection or manual wiring needed — just declare the service as a parameter.
